sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Tottenham Hotspur are struggling, with no wins in their last 4 matches, managing only 1 victory in their last 5 (1-0 vs Crystal Palace). West Ham United are in even worse shape, with no wins in their last 5 matches, conceding heavily and failing to keep a clean sheet in 19 consecutive games. This gives Tottenham a slight edge at home despite their inconsistent form.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters, Tottenham have been dominant, securing a 3-0 win away at West Ham in September 2025 and drawing 1-1 in May 2025. Tottenham are unbeaten in their last 4 matches against West Ham, with both teams often scoring, as seen in 5 of the last 6 head-to-heads.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Tottenham (14th, 27 points) have scored 30 goals but underperformed their expected goals by -9.52, indicating finishing issues, though their defense is mid-table (27 goals conceded). West Ham (18th, 14 points) have struggled offensively and defensively, with 22 goals scored and 43 conceded, showing vulnerability at the back.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Tottenham’s Cristian Romero (7.17 avg rating) and Micky van de Ven (3 goals) will be crucial, though they miss key attackers like Richarlison. West Ham rely on Jarrod Bowen (6 goals), but absences like Lucas Paquetá could hamper their creativity.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Tottenham’s home advantage, superior head-to-head record, and West Ham’s poor defensive form, the Sofascore prediction of a 58% chance for a home win seems plausible. Expect a competitive match with potential for goals on both sides, but Tottenham are likely to capitalize on West Ham’s defensive frailties.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, West Ham United lead Tottenham Hotspur by a narrow margin of 0-1 at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. The away side struck early through Crysencio Summerville in the 15th minute, capitalizing on their opportunity to take the lead. Tottenham, despite dominating possession, have struggled to convert their chances into goals, missing key opportunities. West Ham, on the other hand, have been clinical with their limited openings, making the most of their attacking moments.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Tottenham Hotspur have shown more intent in terms of ball control and territory, spending considerable time in the final third. However, their inability to finish big chances has left them trailing. Defensively, they’ve been somewhat vulnerable, with West Ham finding gaps to exploit on the counter. West Ham United, while less dominant in possession, have been more effective in front of goal. Their defensive structure has held firm under pressure, and they’ve managed to win crucial duels to disrupt Tottenham’s rhythm.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player contributions
halftimeSections.text: For West Ham, Crysencio Summerville has been the standout performer, not only scoring the crucial goal but also posing a constant threat in attack. His ability to create chances has been pivotal. Mateus Fernandes has also played a key role with his assist and defensive contributions in midfield. On the Tottenham side, players like Cristian Romero have been solid at the back with important clearances, while Wilson Odobert and Djed Spence have shown promise in attack but failed to convert their big chances into goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Tottenham starting strongly with consistent attacking pressure in the opening minutes. They had several promising spells early on, pushing forward with intent. However, around the quarter-hour mark, West Ham gained momentum, leading to their goal with a sharp attacking surge. After conceding, Tottenham struggled to regain their initial dominance but had a notable spell of sustained pressure midway through the half. West Ham, meanwhile, enjoyed dangerous bursts late in the half, maintaining a threat on the counter. Towards the end, momentum fluctuated between the two sides with neither able to establish complete control.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Disciplinary and tactical concerns
halftimeSections.text: Discipline has been an issue for Tottenham, with more yellow cards accumulated compared to West Ham. This could impact their approach in the second half, as they risk further bookings or even a sending-off if they continue with aggressive challenges. Tactically, Tottenham may need to sharpen their finishing and consider substitutions to inject fresh energy into their attack. West Ham will likely focus on maintaining their defensive solidity while looking to exploit counter-attacking opportunities to extend their lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half promises to be an intriguing battle as Tottenham will push hard to equalize, leveraging their home advantage and possession-based style. They are likely to come out with renewed vigor, aiming to capitalize on their final third entries. West Ham, however, will aim to protect their lead, possibly sitting deeper to absorb pressure while seeking chances to strike on the break. Given Tottenham’s urgency and West Ham’s clinical edge so far, the game could see more goals as spaces open up. The outcome may hinge on whether Tottenham can convert their chances or if West Ham can maintain their defensive resilience.
